channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: ICG: Improving Cover Image Generation via MLLM-based Prompting and Personalized Preference Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2605.27374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) and diffusion models (DMs) have opened new possibilities for AI-generated content. Yet, personalized cover image generation remains underexplored, despite its critical role in boosting user e...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, model, multimodal, experiment, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LCO: LLM-based Constraint Optimization for Safer Agentic LLMs in Real-world Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2605.27375v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly acting as autonomous agents, but their continuous interaction with the environment can lead to in-context reward hacking (ICRH), a phenomenon where LLMs iteratively optimize their behavior to maximize prox...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, LLM, framework, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Unlocking Fine-Grained and Within-Utterance Speaking Style Control in Prompt-Based Text-to-Speech Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.27376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While prompt-based text-to-speech (TTS) models enable natural language-driven speaking style control, they often provide limited fine-grained control and apply a single global style across an utterance. This restricts practical use cases that require ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, embedding, paper, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RAG-Coding: Enhancing LLM Medical Coding with Structured External Knowledge
item.description: arXiv:2605.27377v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present RAG-Coding, an agentic method for automated ICD-10-CM coding. RAG-Coding orchestrates four large language model (LLM) agents and grounds their coding decisions in external knowledge sources (e.g. the official coding tabular list and guideli...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, RAG, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OralAgent: Integrating Reasoning, Tools, and Knowledge for Interactive Dental Image Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2605.27378v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dental image analysis plays a pivotal role in supporting accurate diagnosis and treatment planning in oral healthcare. Although recent advances have produced dental AI models for specific tasks and individual imaging modalities, their isolated designs...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, multimodal, experiment, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BioELX: Cross-lingual Biomedical Entity Linking via Alias-based Retrieval and LLM Ranking
item.description: arXiv:2605.27380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual biomedical entity linking (BEL) maps mentions in any language to unique identifiers in a biomedical knowledge base (KB), supporting clinical and biomedical NLP applications. However, expert-annotated training data for BEL are costly, esp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, LLM, framework, RAG, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bridging the Stability-Expressivity Gap: Synthetic Data Scaling and Preference Alignment for Low-Resource Spoken Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.27383v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spoken Language Models (SLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for speech synthesis by bypassing explicit grapheme-to-phoneme pipelines. However, their effectiveness in low-resource languages remains fundamentally limited by the scarcity of transc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, framework, alignment, zero-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From AR to Diffusion: Efficiently Adapting Large Language Models with Strictly Causal and Elastic Horizons
item.description: arXiv:2605.27387v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion models promise efficient parallel text generation but rely on bidirectional attention, creating a structural mismatch with pre-trained Autoregressive (AR) models. This incompatibility precludes reusing robust AR priors, necessitating prohibi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, framework, alignment, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Modeling Community Attitude through Reaction Tone: A Human-AI Collaborative Framework for Evaluating LLM Alignment with Linguistic Behaviors in Online Communities
item.description: arXiv:2605.27388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly utilized as proxies for computational social analysis; yet, their ability to faithfully represent the "thick descriptions" (Geertz, 1973) of human communities remains a critical challenge. Current evaluati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, framework, alignment, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EvoSpec: Evolving Speculative Decoding via Real-Time Vocabulary and Parameter AdaptationTarget
item.description: arXiv:2605.27390v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates Large Language Model inference via a draft-then-verify paradigm, yet the output projection layer becomes a bottleneck as vocabulary sizes scale. While existing static pruning methods effectively reduce this overhead, t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, framework, alignment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Identifying and Understanding Human Values in Text: A Tailorable LLM-based Architecture
item.description: arXiv:2605.27373v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As intelligent systems become more autonomous, the scientific community focuses on creating decision-making mechanisms that include ethical and moral considerations, unlike traditional utility-maximisation models. To achieve this, a key aspect is asse...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, prompt engineering, experiment, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Soro: A Lightweight Foundation Model and Chatbot for Tajik
item.description: arXiv:2605.27379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Soro, a family of Tajik-specialized conversational large language models (LLMs) designed for real-world deployment under tight compute and connectivity constraints in Tajikistan. Starting from open-weight Gemma 3 checkpoints, we perform Taj...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, instruction, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DynaSchedBench: Calibrated Dynamic Scheduling Benchmarks and Observability Paradox in LLM-based Scheduling Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.27566v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Progress in neural combinatorial optimization for Dynamic Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Problem (DFJSP) is currently hindered by a methodological tension: static benchmarks encourage benchmark overfitting, while uncalibrated generators obscure algorith...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, framework, augmented, RAG, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why LLMs Fail at Causal Discovery and How Interventional Agents Escape
item.description: arXiv:2605.27567v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal discovery is a cornerstone of scientific reasoning, yet whether large language models can perform it reliably remains an open question. Recent benchmarks show that even fine-tuned models plateau on simple causal graphs and degrade as complexity...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, reasoning, fine-tuning, in-context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LaneRoPE: Positional Encoding for Collaborative Parallel Reasoning and Generation
item.description: arXiv:2605.27570v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parallel LLM test-time scaling techniques (e.g., best-of-$N$) require drawing $N>1$ sequences conditioned on the same input prompt. These methods boost accuracy while exploiting the computational efficiency of batching $N$ generations. However, each s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, LLM, paper, reasoning, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Discovery Agents for Real-Time Analytics: Toward Proactive Insight Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.27571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern analytics systems are fundamentally reactive, requiring users to define queries over increasingly complex and continuously evolving data. In real-time streaming environments, this paradigm breaks down, as the space of potential insights becomes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: You Are in Control of Your State: Why Human Outcomes Are Controllable Through Causal State Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2605.27580v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central puzzle for the behavioural sciences and for human-facing artificial intelligence is the persistence of within-person variability. The same individual, presented with the same observable input, produces different outcomes on different occasio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, framework, research, attention, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: $E^3$-Agent: An Executable and Evolving Agent for Resource Management of Edge Generative Inference
item.description: arXiv:2605.27428v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Edge deployments of generative inference increasingly face two practical realities: per-device per-model performance is often unknown at deployment time, and it is non-stationary due to user-driven semantic events, background load, and device churn. C...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, model, LLM, paper, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Comparative Analysis of Liquid Neural Networks and LSTM for Sequential Pattern Recognition: Robustness, Efficiency, and Clinical Utility
item.description: arXiv:2605.27467v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditional Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units operate on discrete time steps, often failing to capture the fluid temporal dynamics of real-world physical processes. Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs), specifically Clo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, memory, study, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Detect by Yourself: Self-Designing Agentic Workflows for Few-Shot Graph Anomaly Detection
item.description: arXiv:2605.27470v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph anomaly detection aims to identify anomaly nodes in attributed graphs and plays an important role in real-world applications. However, existing graph anomaly detection methods still face two key challenges: 1) fixed pipelines, which restrict the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, vision, paper, framework, few-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt engineering, prompt, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, framework, reasoning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: IGADA-IoT: IoT Sensor Energy Optimization in Wireless Sensor Networks Driven by Automatic Data Augmentation
item.description: arXiv:2605.27397v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In wireless sensor networks (WSNs), data augmentation is a novel method to improve sampling-frequency decision performance, thereby enabling energy optimization for IoT (Internet of Things) sensors. However, existing methods rely on a single generator...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, framework, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Tackling Multimodal Learning Challenges with Mixture-of-Expert: A Survey
item.description: arXiv:2605.27431v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) presents a naturally compatible and scalable framework for multimodal learning, demonstrating strong adaptability across diverse modalities and tasks. Despite its growing success, a comprehensive and systematic review on the M...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, multimodal, framework, research, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Personalized Observation Normalization for Federated Reinforcement Learning in Simulation Environments with Heterogeneity
item.description: arXiv:2605.27385v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated reinforcement learning (FedRL) enables multiple agents to collaboratively train a global policy without sharing raw data, making it ideal for privacy-sensitive applications. However, FedRL faces challenges in heterogeneous environments where...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Metric-Aware PCA as a Linear Instance of Geometric Deep Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.27456v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometric deep learning organises neural architectures around the symmetries of their data domain, with the choice of symmetry group serving as a geometric prior that determines what representations can be learned. Metric-Aware Principal Component Ana...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HEAL: Resilient and Self-* Hub-based Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.27475v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized learning enhances privacy, scalability, and fault tolerance by distributing data and computation across nodes. A popular approach is Federated learning, which relies on a central aggregator, yet faces challenges such as server vulnerabil...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, step-by-step, example, prompt, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: WrenAI - Give AI agents the context to query business data correctly through the open context layer that gives AI agents grounded, governed memory, context, SQL across 20+ data sources, that helps you build GenBI, agentic BI, text-to-sql, dashboards, and agentic analytics.

item.title: On the Origin of Synthetic Information by Means of Steganographic Inheritance
item.description: arXiv:2605.27551v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The origin of species has been the mystery of mysteries in natural science. By analogy, the origin of synthetic information, we suggest, is the mystery of mysteries in information science. The question carries a moral weight that a technical account c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, analysis, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agyn: An Open-Source Platform for AI Agents with Scalable On-Demand Execution, Agent Definition as a Code, and Zero-Trust Access
item.description: arXiv:2605.27575v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As organizations move toward production deployments of AI agents, which execute non-deterministic workflows, maintain stateful sessions, and often operate with privileged access to internal services, the engineering challenge shifts from building indi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, model, paper, product, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
